title: Founding Engineer
description: Architecting and building a full-stack platform from zero — spanning product infrastructure, design systems, and a modular composable widget layer across a 50+ package monorepo.
highlights: Built a Turborepo monorepo with 50+ packages across domain, UI, and platform layers using Next.js and TypeScript
Designed a modular widget system enabling dynamic, composable interfaces and rapid feature iteration
Developed core product infrastructure: command palette, theming system, and extensible sidebar navigation
Established scalable engineering foundations — PNPM workspaces, shared configs, and internal documentation
Led end-to-end product decisions across architecture, design system, and UX, prioritizing long-term extensibility

title: Software Engineer II
description: Built and maintained internal tooling for the Merchandising org — improving data reliability, shipping user-driven features, and leading a full rebuild of a core internal app.
highlights: Reduced Merch team workload by 300+ hours per month through targeted code optimisations and automation
Rebuilt a core internal web app using React and TypeScript, improving scalability, maintainability, and UX
Partnered across Merch, IT, and Product teams to resolve complex data issues and improve system reliability
Designed and deployed features based on direct user feedback, iterating quickly on real workflows

title: Apprentice Software Engineer
description: Contributed to consumer-facing social sharing features on the Pandora web platform, working closely with product, design, and the core web team.
highlights: Led development of social sharing features end-to-end — from ideation through production
Collaborated with product, design, and platform teams using React, TypeScript, and GraphQL
Received intensive hands-on training while contributing directly to key product initiatives

title: Lead Instructor
description: Delivered full-stack curriculum and technical mentorship to 40+ students across cohorts, covering JavaScript, React, Redux, Ruby on Rails, and modern developer workflows.
highlights: Mentored 40+ students through 5 projects each — guiding architecture, debugging, and deployment
Delivered live lectures across the full stack: JavaScript, React, Redux, Ruby on Rails, and ActiveRecord
Improved curriculum delivery by incorporating real-world coding challenges and modern tooling
